name: Kubeflow Trainer
tagline: Kubernetes-native distributed training and LLM fine-tuning across PyTorch, JAX, and more
seoTitle: Kubeflow Trainer: Distributed Training on Kubernetes
metaDescription: Kubeflow Trainer is a Kubernetes operator for distributed model training and LLM fine-tuning across PyTorch, JAX, DeepSpeed, and XGBoost, driven from a Python SDK.
overview: Kubeflow Trainer is a Kubernetes-native platform for distributed AI training and large language model (LLM) fine-tuning. It runs multi-node, multi-GPU jobs across many frameworks, including PyTorch, MLX, HuggingFace, DeepSpeed, JAX, and XGBoost, and exposes them through two Kubernetes APIs: TrainJob and Runtimes.

It is built for teams that already run on Kubernetes and need to scale training beyond a single machine. It brings MPI to Kubernetes to coordinate high-throughput communication between GPU nodes, and integrates with ecosystem projects like Kueue for topology-aware scheduling and JobSet and LeaderWorkerSet for workload orchestration.

As a compute-orchestration tool, it sits between your training code and the cluster. AI practitioners define jobs in Python with the Kubeflow SDK, while the operator handles pod creation, scheduling, and a distributed data cache that streams large datasets toward GPU nodes.
features: Distributed training and LLM fine-tuning across PyTorch, MLX, HuggingFace, DeepSpeed, JAX, and XGBoost
Two Kubernetes APIs, TrainJob and Runtimes, for defining and reusing training setups
MPI on Kubernetes for multi-node, multi-GPU jobs on HPC clusters
Integrates with Kueue for topology-aware scheduling and with JobSet and LeaderWorkerSet for orchestration
Distributed data cache that streams large datasets to GPU nodes with zero-copy transfer
Python SDK (TrainerClient, CustomTrainer) to launch and manage jobs without writing Kubernetes manifests

gettingStarted.intro: Kubeflow Trainer has two parts: a control plane you install on a Kubernetes cluster, and a Python SDK you use to submit jobs. You need Kubernetes and kubectl 1.31 or newer.

steps.heading: Install the control plane
steps.body: Apply the Trainer manifests to your cluster with kubectl. Set VERSION to a released tag.

steps.heading: Verify the pods are running
steps.body: Check that the JobSet and Trainer controller managers are up in the kubeflow-system namespace.

steps.heading: Install the Python SDK
steps.body: Install the Kubeflow SDK to talk to the Trainer APIs from Python.

steps.heading: Submit a TrainJob
steps.body: List the available runtimes, then launch a CustomTrainer job with TrainerClient. Replace train_pytorch with your own training function.

useCases: Fine-tune an LLM across several GPU nodes on a shared Kubernetes cluster
Run distributed PyTorch or JAX training jobs without writing raw Kubernetes manifests
Schedule multi-node MPI training on HPC hardware with queueing through Kueue
Migrate existing Kubeflow Training Operator V1 workloads to the newer TrainJob API
